item.description: El delantero aprovechó una recuperación tras una presión alta para marcar el primer tanto de la Copa del Mundo 2026.

media:content.media:title: Julián Quiñones anota el primer gol del Mundial para México
media:content.media:description: El delantero aprovechó una recuperación tras una presión alta para marcar el primer tanto de la Copa del Mundo 2026.

item.title: Julián Quiñones anota el primer gol del Mundial para México

item.description: El delantero apareció a segundo palo para rematar frente a la portería. El ex americanista marcó su primer gol en Copas del Mundo.

media:content.media:title: Raúl Jiménez anota el 2-0 con dedicación al cielo
media:content.media:description: El delantero apareció a segundo palo para rematar frente a la portería. El ex americanista marcó su primer gol en Copas del Mundo.

item.title: Raúl Jiménez anota el 2-0 con dedicación al cielo

item.description: President Trump said an agreement to end the war could be signed with the Iranian regime within days, but that some thorny issues would remain for later talks. NBC News’ Gabe Gutierrez reports.

media:content.media:title: Trump says deal with Iran could be signed in days
media:content.media:description: President Trump said an agreement to end the war could be signed with the Iranian regime within days, but that some thorny issues would remain for later talks. NBC News’ Gabe Gutierrez reports.

item.title: Trump says deal with Iran could be signed in days

item.dc:creator: Alexander Smith
item.description: The island less than 20 miles off Iran accounts for more than 90% of the country's oil exports.

media:content.media:text: Satellite Imagery Of Kharg Island In Iran
media:content.media:description: Satellite view of Kharg Island in the Persian Gulf off the coast of Iran.

media:content.media:text: IRAN-ECONOMY-OIL-KHARK
media:content.media:description: An oil facility on Kharg Island in 2017.

media:content.media:text: Pipes leading downhill toward the Kharg Island jetty in Iran from the 17 million-barrel-capacity tank farm in 1971.
media:content.media:description: Pipes leading downhill toward the Kharg Island jetty in Iran from the 17 million-barrel-capacity tank farm in 1971.

media:content.media:text: USS Tripoli (LHA-7) amphibious assault enters the Singapore Strait
media:content.media:description: The USS Tripoli amphibious assault ship enters the Singapore Strait amid the U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran as seen from Singapore on March 17.

item.title: Why Trump’s eyes are on Iran’s Kharg Island — and why that's a risk

item.dc:creator: Steve Kopack
Allie Canal
item.description: The company controlled by Elon Musk will begin trading Friday at a valuation of $1.77 trillion.

media:content.media:text: SpaceX facilities.
media:content.media:description: SpaceX facilities in Hawthorne, Calif., on April 13, 2026.

item.title: SpaceX locks in IPO price of $135, making it largest stock debut ever

item.dc:creator: Rebecca Shabad
Sarah Dean
Dan De Luce
item.description: The president had named Pulte to hold the Cabinet position temporarily after Tulsi Gabbard resigned from the post.

media:content.media:text: Jay Clayton.
media:content.media:description: Jay Clayton, US attorney for the Southern District of New York (SDNY), during the Bloomberg Global Credit Forum in New York, US, on Wednesday, June 3, 2026.

item.title: Trump nominates Jay Clayton as director of national intelligence

item.dc:creator: Joelle Gross
Rohan Nadkarni
item.description: The Spurs-Knicks games so far have been decided by an average of 4 points. The average margin across NBA Finals series is 11.8 points.

media:content.media:text: OG Anunoby makes a game-winning tip shot against San Antonio Spurs guards Dylan Harper and Devin Vassell on the basketball court
media:content.media:description: The moment after New York Knicks forward OG Anunoby made a game-winning tip shot against the San Antonio Spurs during Game 4 of the NBA Finals on Wednesday. The Knicks won by 1 point, the second 1-point game of the series.

item.title: Data shows just how close these NBA Finals games have been

item.dc:creator: Melanie Zanona
Katherine Doyle
item.description: President Donald Trump has taken political revenge on all four Republicans who signed on to the Epstein files release law.

media:content.media:text: Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-GA) speaks at a news conference alongside Rep. Thomas Massie (R-KY) at the U.S. Capitol Building on May 01, 2024 in Washington, DC.
media:content.media:description: GOP Reps. Marjorie Taylor Greene and Thomas Massie both drew President Donald Trump's ire over the Jeffrey Epstein files and other issues.

item.title: Republicans who pushed Epstein files release see political careers upended by Trump

item.dc:creator: Steve Kopack
item.description: Wholesale inflation surged to its highest level since late 2022, as fuel prices pushed higher by the war with Iran ripple across the economy.

media:content.media:text: CORRECTION / US-POLITICS-TRUMP
media:content.media:description: President Donald in New York City on Tuesday.

item.title: The inflation surge is hitting businesses, too

item.dc:creator: Nicole Acevedo
item.description: Local businesses have taken a hit as families and workers grapple with the effects of the raid last June on the Glenn Valley Foods meatpacking plant, community members said.

media:content.media:text: The Glenn Valley Foods facility Omaha, Neb., on June 13, 2025.
media:content.media:description: Federal immigration authorities arrested 76 people working at Glenn Valley Foods in Omaha, Neb., in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: Roger Garcia speaks at a podium outside, facing away from the street, some people sit behind him
media:content.media:description: Federal immigration authorities arrested 76 people working at Glenn Valley Foods in Omaha, Neb., in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: South Omaha’s business district, known as a vibrant Hispanic enclave, is still feeling the impacts of last year's immigration raid, officials and business owners say.
media:content.media:description: Federal immigration authorities arrested 76 people working at Glenn Valley Foods in Omaha, Neb., in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: Luis Mejia speaks into a microphone being held in front of him
media:content.media:description: Federal immigration authorities arrested 76 people working at Glenn Valley Foods in Omaha, Neb., in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: A diverse crowd of mostly Omaha residents protested workplace immigration raids in June 2025.
media:content.media:description: Federal immigration authorities arrested 76 people working at Glenn Valley Foods in Omaha, Neb., in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: Meatpacking employees at Glenn Valley Foods in Omaha in June 2025.
media:content.media:description: Federal immigration authorities arrested 76 people working at Glenn Valley Foods in Omaha, Neb., in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: Demonstrators in Omaha after immigration raids in June 2025.
media:content.media:description: Federal immigration authorities arrested 76 people working at Glenn Valley Foods in Omaha, Neb., in June 2025.

item.title: A Nebraska immigration raid shut businesses down a year ago. The fallout is ongoing, officials say.

item.description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

media:content.media:title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final
media:content.media:description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

item.title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final

item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang
item.description: The body of a 5-year-old girl who was swept into the ocean at California’s Laguna Beach this week has been recovered, officials said Thursday.

media:content.media:text: California High Surf Missing Girl
media:content.media:description: A lifeguard rescue boat cruises around the area where a child was swept out to sea near the Montage Resort on Wednesday, June 10, 2026, in Laguna Beach, Calif.

item.title: Body of 5-year-old girl who was swept out to sea at California’s Laguna Beach is found

item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
item.description: LONDON — U.K. Defense Secretary John Healey unexpectedly quit on Thursday, saying the government is unwilling to spend enough on the military at a time of “rising threats.”

media:content.media:text: Cabinet Meeting in Downing Street in London
media:content.media:description: Secretary of State for Defense John Healey leaves 10 Downing Street after attending the weekly Cabinet meeting in London on June 2.

item.title: U.K. defense secretary resigns, saying the government isn’t willing to spend enough on the military

item.dc:creator: Jared Perlo
item.description: Anthropic’s latest AI model might be the company’s most powerful public release, but the system’s strict safety measures quickly triggered some of the strongest backlash the AI giant has faced

media:content.media:text: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei, right, and Chief Product Officer Mike Krieger talk after unveiling Claude 4 during the Code with Claude conference on Thursday, May 22, 2025 in San Francisco.
media:content.media:description: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei, right, and Chief Product Officer Mike Krieger in 2025 in San Francisco.

item.title: Anthropic rankles users with safety-first Fable release

item.dc:creator: David K. Li
item.description: “X-Men” actor and former wrestler Tyler Mane’s announcement that he has breast cancer may prompt men to increase their vigilance about a disease closely associated with women, doctors say

media:content.media:text: Tyler Mane
media:content.media:description: Tyler Mane attends the "Deadpool & Wolverine" New York City premiere on July 22, 2024.

item.title: ‘X-Men’ star Tyler Mane’s openness about his breast cancer could reduce stigma, doctors say

item.dc:creator: Sahil Kapur
Brennan Leach
Frank Thorp V
Kyle Stewart
item.description: FISA Section 702, a warrantless surveillance tool, is set to lapse as Congress fights with President Donald Trump about Bill Pulte, the acting Director of National Intelligence.

media:content.media:text: U.S. Capitol Building Exterior
media:content.media:description: Congress is heading out of town, having not renewed FISA Section 702 on time for the first time in its history.

item.title: FISA spying power scheduled to expire as House fails to extend it before leaving town

item.dc:creator: Nicole Acevedo
item.description: Local businesses have taken a hit as families and workers grapple with the effects of the raid last June on the Glenn Valley Foods meatpacking plant, community members said.

media:content.media:text: The Glenn Valley Foods facility Omaha, Neb., on June 13, 2025.
media:content.media:description: Federal immigration authorities arrested 76 people working at Glenn Valley Foods in Omaha, Neb., in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: Roger Garcia speaks at a podium outside, facing away from the street, some people sit behind him
media:content.media:description: Federal immigration authorities arrested 76 people working at Glenn Valley Foods in Omaha, Neb., in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: South Omaha’s business district, known as a vibrant Hispanic enclave, is still feeling the impacts of last year's immigration raid, officials and business owners say.
media:content.media:description: Federal immigration authorities arrested 76 people working at Glenn Valley Foods in Omaha, Neb., in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: Luis Mejia speaks into a microphone being held in front of him
media:content.media:description: Federal immigration authorities arrested 76 people working at Glenn Valley Foods in Omaha, Neb., in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: A diverse crowd of mostly Omaha residents protested workplace immigration raids in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: Demonstrators in Omaha after immigration raids in June 2025.
media:content.media:description: Federal immigration authorities arrested 76 people working at Glenn Valley Foods in Omaha, Neb., in June 2025.

item.title: A Nebraska immigration raid shut businesses down a year ago. The fallout is ongoing, officials say.

item.dc:creator: Evan Bush
item.description: Prepare for heat, drought and flooding — it’s officially El Niño season, according to the National Weather Service. This one could be on par with some of the strongest.

media:content.media:text: Extreme heat warning in New York
media:content.media:description: People walk along the Brooklyn Bridge during an extreme heat warning in New York in July 2025.

item.title: El Niño is officially here, and this one could be a doozy

item.dc:creator: Alicia Victoria Lozano
item.description: Said Abdullahi Ereg, who sat atop the FBI’s “Most Wanted Fraudster” list, is accused of stealing millions from the Federal Children’s Nutrition Program.

media:content.media:text: The logo of the Federal Bureau of Investigation hung on a blue wall next to an American flag
media:content.media:description: Said Abdullahi Ereg turned himself in this week to the FBI. He faces several charges, including conspiracy to commit wire fraud, wire fraud and money laundering.

item.title: Minneapolis man indicted in $4 million pandemic fraud case turns himself in, officials say

item.dc:creator: The Politics Desk
item.description: As the World Cup kicks off today, Monica Alba explores the intersection between the tournament and the ongoing war with Iran.

media:content.media:text: Iranian national football team's training
media:content.media:description: Iran's national football team continues its training camp ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup on May 20, 2026.

item.title: When the World Cup and war collide: From the Politics Desk

item.dc:creator: Rebecca Cohen
item.description: A man was arrested outside of an Olive Garden in New Hampshire after a woman said he was holding her hostage, had forced her to marry him and conducted a “satanic ritual” on her, police said.

media:content.media:text: A New Hampshire State Police badge on a sleeve.
media:content.media:description: A New Hampshire State Police badge.

item.title: Woman rescued at Olive Garden after man forced her into ‘satanic ritual’ and held her hostage, police say

item.dc:creator: Erika Edwards
Maggie Vespa
item.description: Florida health officials are pushing back against quarantine guidance from the CDC for passengers from the cruise ship MV Hondius exposed to the Andes hantavirus.

media:content.media:text: First passengers leave HV Hondius amid suspected hantavirus concerns in Tenerife
media:content.media:description: The first passengers from the MV Hondius depart for Tenerife's airport aboard a Spanish military emergency unit bus at Granadilla Port in Tenerife, Canary Islands, on May 10.

media:content.media:text: Angela Perryman stands outside near a cliff by the ocean
media:content.media:description: The first passengers from the MV Hondius depart for Tenerife's airport aboard a Spanish military emergency unit bus at Granadilla Port in Tenerife, Canary Islands, on May 10.

media:content.media:text: This aerial picture shows a general view of the cruise ship MV Hondius stationary off the port of Praia
media:content.media:description: The first passengers from the MV Hondius depart for Tenerife's airport aboard a Spanish military emergency unit bus at Granadilla Port in Tenerife, Canary Islands, on May 10.

item.title: CDC and Florida at odds over hantavirus cruise ship passenger’s quarantine

item.dc:creator: Steve Kopack
Allie Canal
item.description: The company controlled by Elon Musk will begin trading Friday at a valuation of $1.77 trillion.

media:content.media:text: SpaceX facilities.

item.title: SpaceX locks in IPO price of $135, making it largest stock debut ever

item.dc:creator: David K. Li
item.description: “There is little I can think of more disturbing than the innocence of a children’s lemonade stand being violated by an armed robbery,” a City Council member said.

media:content.media:text: Two suspects.
media:content.media:description: Police are searching for two people who robbed a children's lemonade stand in South Boston at gunpoint Wednesday.

item.title: Boston lemonade stand robbed at gunpoint; robbers flee and remain at large

item.dc:creator: Corky Siemaszko
item.description: The inadvertent shooting was caught on dashcam, and the wounded officer has recovered, the Pasadena police chief said.

media:content.media:text: Pasadena Police Department.
media:content.media:description: Pasadena Police Department.

item.title: Video shows California cop accidentally shooting fellow cop in ‘horseplay’ incident
